title: Dennis Takes a Mental Health Day

jokes.text: Pills are for old men.
jokes.setup: Doctor recommends blood pressure medication for Dennis's health
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismisses medication with toxic masculinity logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - his vanity and delusion about aging drives the humor. Economic delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Pills are for old men.

jokes.text: If I wanted my blood pressure to be low, I would simply command my body to make it low.
jokes.setup: Doctor explains how blood pressure medication works
jokes.punchline: Dennis believes he can control his body through sheer willpower
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Dennis's god complex. The word 'simply' makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If I wanted my blood pressure to be low, I would simply command my body to make it low.

jokes.text: I'm chronically suffering fools who won't listen to me when I say that I can do things.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to explain his high blood pressure
jokes.punchline: He blames others for not believing in his impossible claims
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic worldview on full display - everyone else is the problem, never him.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm chronically suffering fools who won't listen to me when I say that I can do things.

jokes.text: Now I'll have proof I can smash in that stupid doctor's face.
jokes.setup: Dennis setting up his blood pressure monitoring equipment
jokes.punchline: Casual threat of violence against his doctor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of Dennis - casual violence threat delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now I'll have proof I can smash in that stupid doctor's face.

jokes.text: Well, we can't be below sea level. Otherwise, we'd be underwater.
jokes.setup: Mac asking if they're above or below sea level for pressure cooker safety
jokes.punchline: Dennis states the obvious with deadpan logic
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor - Dennis being the voice of reason in an absurd conversation.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, we can't be below sea level. Otherwise, we'd be underwater.

jokes.text: You're telling me that you can't bring a pressure cooker onto a submarine? How do they feed the entire U.S. Navy?
jokes.setup: Discussion about pressure cookers and sea level
jokes.punchline: Mac escalates to submarines and Navy logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - taking a simple concept and spiraling into military conspiracy territory.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're telling me that you can't bring a pressure cooker onto a submarine? How do they feed the entire U.S. Navy?

jokes.text: Navy argument.
jokes.setup: Mac's submarine pressure cooker theory
jokes.punchline: They treat 'Navy argument' as if it's a legitimate debate tactic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - two words that perfectly capture their absurd logic system.

dialogue.line: Navy argument.

jokes.text: What kind of cuckoo shit is that?
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions taking a mental health day
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissive reaction to the concept
jokes.explanation: Solid character reaction - dismissive attitude toward mental health concepts fits the show's tone.

dialogue.line: What kind of cuckoo shit is that?

jokes.text: It's like a sick day but for your brain, and if somebody calls it, you have to give it to 'em, you can't ask any questions.
jokes.setup: Explaining what a mental health day is
jokes.punchline: The 'no questions' rule makes it sound like a legal loophole
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about modern workplace concepts presented as immutable law.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's like a sick day but for your brain, and if somebody calls it, you have to give it to 'em, you can't ask any questions.

jokes.text: This could be a good way to find out whether or not we're, uh, underwater.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting to go to the beach
jokes.punchline: Mac circles back to the underwater question with sincere concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Mac is still genuinely concerned about being underwater.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This could be a good way to find out whether or not we're, uh, underwater.

jokes.text: That means stop. No!
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to escape for his mental health day
jokes.punchline: His blood pressure monitor literally tells him to stop
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - his attempt to lower stress is immediately contradicted by his monitoring device.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That means stop. No!

jokes.text: Wow. That's a lot of typing you're having to do there. You writing a novel?
jokes.setup: Car rental employee typing at computer
jokes.punchline: Dennis makes an awkward observation about normal computer work
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor - Dennis being socially awkward while trying to be charming.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wow. That's a lot of typing you're having to do there. You writing a novel?

jokes.text: keys are sort of a system that have existed for millennia. I don't know why we're trying to fix that
jokes.setup: Learning the car uses an app instead of keys
jokes.punchline: Dennis treats keys as ancient wisdom being foolishly abandoned
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Dennis treating a minor inconvenience as an assault on civilization.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: keys are sort of a system that have existed for millennia. I don't know why we're trying to fix that

jokes.text: we got apps for everything, right? Even for things we already had things for.
jokes.setup: Frustrated about downloading another app
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the redundancy of app-ifying everything
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor about modern technology redundancy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: we got apps for everything, right? Even for things we already had things for.

jokes.text: My good friend, Mr. Kratom.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting to relax on his mental health day
jokes.punchline: Personifying kratom as a friend shows his relationship with substances
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Dennis's casual drug use presented with false intimacy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: My good friend, Mr. Kratom.

jokes.text: Rerouting to Teaspoon.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks car to find nearest tea shop
jokes.punchline: Car immediately responds with perfect efficiency
jokes.explanation: Sets up the irony that car AI works perfectly while everything else fails.

dialogue.speaker: Car AI
dialogue.line: Rerouting to Teaspoon.

jokes.text: covert diabetes delivery mechanism
jokes.setup: Boba tea employee explaining tapioca pearls
jokes.punchline: Dennis describes boba as a conspiracy to give people diabetes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant phrase that's both accurate and absurd. Perfect Dennis paranoia expressed with clinical precision.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: covert diabetes delivery mechanism

jokes.text: The system won't let me do that.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking for tea without boba
jokes.punchline: Employee can't override the system for a simple request
jokes.explanation: Continues the episode's theme of systems controlling people instead of serving them.

dialogue.speaker: Boba employee
dialogue.line: The system won't let me do that.

jokes.text: Self-care, right? Isn't that what people call it whenever they need an excuse to do whatever the fuck they want?
jokes.setup: Dennis ordering sugary drink on his 'mental health day'
jokes.punchline: Cynical take on self-care culture as excuse-making
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary that's both cynical and self-aware - Dennis calling out the thing he's doing.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Self-care, right? Isn't that what people call it whenever they need an excuse to do whatever the fuck they want?

jokes.text: Of course you don't. Of course you don't.
jokes.setup: Boba employee says 'I don't know' about self-care
jokes.punchline: Dennis condescends to minimum wage worker for not having philosophical opinions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis behavior - being condescending to someone just trying to do their job.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Of course you don't. Of course you don't.

jokes.text: One chewy sugar tea, please.
jokes.setup: Dennis's frustration with the boba tea concept
jokes.punchline: Describes boba tea in the most unappetizing way possible
jokes.explanation: Great passive-aggressive description that makes boba sound disgusting while being technically accurate.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: One chewy sugar tea, please.

jokes.text: I have cash, you have tea. Why don't we just streamline things?
jokes.setup: Employee wants him to scan QR code for app
jokes.punchline: Dennis reduces transaction to basic bartering logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of frustration - Dennis appealing to prehistoric commerce to avoid modern technology.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I have cash, you have tea. Why don't we just streamline things?

jokes.text: There's no minimum on the app, though.
jokes.setup: Ten dollar minimum for card payments
jokes.punchline: App conveniently has no minimum, pushing people toward it
jokes.explanation: Reveals the systematic push toward app usage - another small frustration in Dennis's day.

dialogue.speaker: Boba employee
dialogue.line: There's no minimum on the app, though.

jokes.text: we're both victims here, so...
jokes.setup: Dennis apologizing to boba employee
jokes.punchline: Makes himself equal victim to the person he's been condescending to
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis move - false solidarity that still centers himself as the real victim.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: we're both victims here, so...

jokes.text: That is rough. Oh. Ugh! Aah! It's so sweet.
jokes.setup: Dennis finally tries the boba tea he was forced to buy
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction to the sweetness he predicted would be terrible
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the boba setup - Dennis's prediction about 'covert diabetes delivery' proven right.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That is rough. Oh. Ugh! Aah! It's so sweet.

jokes.text: Key not found? Th-There is no key. The key is my... phone.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to get back in the car
jokes.punchline: Realizes the logical flaw in phone-as-key when phone is inside car
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the key/app setup - the exact problem Dennis feared materializes.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Key not found? Th-There is no key. The key is my... phone.

jokes.text: No, that French dip has been in the fridge for a month, dude. Do not eat it and do not call me!
jokes.setup: Gang calling Dennis during his crisis
jokes.punchline: They're calling about eating month-old food
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Dennis's worst moment interrupted by the most trivial concern.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, that French dip has been in the fridge for a month, dude. Do not eat it and do not call me!

jokes.text: We already ate it... It's been eaten.
jokes.setup: Dennis telling them not to eat the old sandwich
jokes.punchline: They've already eaten it and are just informing him
jokes.explanation: Perfect gang logic - asking permission after the fact, delivered with matter-of-fact tone.

dialogue.line: We already ate it... It's been eaten.

jokes.text: I guarantee that the U.S. Navy is down on their submarine...
jokes.setup: Dennis getting angry about the pressure cooking
jokes.punchline: Mac returns to his submarine Navy theory mid-crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character consistency - bringing up his pet theory at the worst possible moment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I guarantee that the U.S. Navy is down on their submarine...

jokes.text: I, uh... I can't because I don't have a key. Uh, well, technically there is no key. My phone is the key, and... I don't have a phone.
jokes.setup: Police officer asking Dennis to turn off the car
jokes.punchline: Dennis has to explain the absurd modern car situation to a cop
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the key/phone problem now involves law enforcement. Dennis's explanation sounds insane.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I, uh... I can't because I don't have a key. Uh, well, technically there is no key. My phone is the key, and... I don't have a phone.

jokes.text: Right. You don't care.
jokes.setup: Cop ordering him out despite his explanation
jokes.punchline: Dennis's defeated realization that logic doesn't matter
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan acceptance of powerlessness. Four words that capture complete defeat.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Right. You don't care.

jokes.text: Hi. Good afternoon. Fine, and you? Thanks for asking.
jokes.setup: Dennis entering phone store in crisis
jokes.punchline: Has imaginary polite conversation with employee who hasn't spoken
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment showing Dennis's fraying mental state through false social niceties.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hi. Good afternoon. Fine, and you? Thanks for asking.

jokes.text: Those aren't the phones? ... those aren't the phones, guys, so, you know, just so you know. Those are just boxes filled with phones that aren't the phones.
jokes.setup: Employee explaining phones are for new customers only
jokes.punchline: Dennis addresses imaginary audience about the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's mental breakdown through addressing an invisible audience like he's in a documentary.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Those aren't the phones? ... those aren't the phones, guys, so, you know, just so you know. Those are just boxes filled with phones that aren't the phones.

jokes.text: Let's punish those people and give the phones that are right there to the new customers.
jokes.setup: Learning loyal customers can't get immediate phone replacement
jokes.punchline: Dennis frames customer service as active punishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of frustration - Dennis interprets corporate policy as personal malice.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Let's punish those people and give the phones that are right there to the new customers.

jokes.text: the system isn't here for me to direct my frustrations at it
jokes.setup: Dennis saying he's not mad at the employee
jokes.punchline: Wants to yell at 'the system' but it's not a person
jokes.explanation: Insightful observation about modern frustrations - wanting to fight an abstract concept.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: the system isn't here for me to direct my frustrations at it

jokes.text: diamonds are made under pressure. This is a pressure cooker. We're going to cook diamonds.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining their diamond-making plan
jokes.punchline: Applies geological process to kitchen appliance with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - takes scientific fact and applies it with completely wrong understanding but total confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: diamonds are made under pressure. This is a pressure cooker. We're going to cook diamonds.

jokes.text: It's very linear.
jokes.setup: Charlie's insane diamond-cooking plan
jokes.punchline: Mac treats it as obviously logical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac response - 'linear' makes the absurd plan sound scientific and reasonable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's very linear.

jokes.text: In my day, if you wanted to get out of work, you... you hit yourself with a hammer or you got gnawed by a shark or something simple.
jokes.setup: Discussing mental health days
jokes.punchline: Frank's old-school approach to skipping work involved self-harm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - presents extreme self-harm as 'the good old days' with the word 'simple' making it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: In my day, if you wanted to get out of work, you... you hit yourself with a hammer or you got gnawed by a shark or something simple.

jokes.setup: Dennis needing Frank's PIN to get a new phone
jokes.punchline: Of course Frank's PIN is 69-69
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank character detail - immature but predictable. Great economy.

jokes.text: Uh, let's try... 69-69.
jokes.setup: Dennis needs PIN for phone plan
jokes.punchline: His guess at Frank's PIN is predictably juvenile
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis knows Frank well enough to guess his immature PIN choice

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, let's try... 69-69.

jokes.text: Why won't you talk to each other?! Talk! Talk! Talk! Talk!
jokes.setup: Dennis's new phone won't sync with his car
jokes.punchline: He yells at inanimate objects like they're refusing to cooperate
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Dennis's complete mental breakdown - anthropomorphizing technology.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why won't you talk to each other?! Talk! Talk! Talk! Talk!

jokes.text: "D" as in "Deliver me from this!" "E" as in "Engage with human." "N" as in "nightmare"!
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to spell his name for automated system
jokes.punchline: Uses increasingly desperate phrases that start with each letter
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation. Each letter becomes more desperate and existential. Perfect structure building to total breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: "D" as in "Deliver me from this!" "E" as in "Engage with human." "N" as in "nightmare"!

jokes.text: My new phone won't talk to my car.
jokes.setup: Dennis beginning to explain his situation to Daisy
jokes.punchline: Reduces complex technology problem to simple relationship issue
jokes.explanation: Good anthropomorphization - makes technology sound like relationship counseling.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: My new phone won't talk to my car.

jokes.text: Just you and me, Daisy, fighting the good fight against a broken system engineered to drive us both so crazy that we have to take days off for our mental health!
jokes.setup: Dennis's long explanation to customer service
jokes.punchline: Connects his mental health day to the system that's destroying his mental health
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Dennis realizes the irony of his situation. Perfect callback to episode premise.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Just you and me, Daisy, fighting the good fight against a broken system engineered to drive us both so crazy that we have to take days off for our mental health!

jokes.text: when he gets back from the beach house.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking to leave feedback for the CEO
jokes.punchline: CEO is at the beach - exactly where Dennis was supposed to be
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - the CEO gets the relaxation Dennis was seeking while Dennis suffers from the CEO's company.

dialogue.line: when he gets back from the beach house.

jokes.text: I was supposed to go to the beach today.
jokes.setup: Learning CEO is at beach house
jokes.punchline: Dennis's quiet realization of the cosmic injustice
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered moment of defeat. The economy and sadness make it both funny and poignant.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I was supposed to go to the beach today.

jokes.text: You lowered your blood pressure by 20 points in less than a minute, but how?
jokes.setup: Dennis back at doctor's office
jokes.punchline: His blood pressure improved dramatically somehow
jokes.explanation: Great ironic payoff - his terrible day somehow achieved the goal of lowering blood pressure.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Tsuma
dialogue.line: You lowered your blood pressure by 20 points in less than a minute, but how?

jokes.text: Well, just let off a little steam, all naturally.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking how Dennis lowered his blood pressure
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of his complete mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - 'let off a little steam' for a complete psychotic break. Classic Dennis false confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, just let off a little steam, all naturally.

jokes.text: are-are we above or below sea level?
jokes.setup: Dennis finally returning to the bar
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately returns to the same obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback - Mac hasn't moved past his original concern despite Dennis's entire ordeal.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: are-are we above or below sea level?

jokes.text: I'll be right in.
jokes.setup: Mac asking the same question that started Dennis's nightmare
jokes.punchline: Dennis calmly accepts his fate and heads back inside
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Dennis's complete surrender to the cyclical nature of his hell. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'll be right in.
